_metadata.note: Written to repository root because the .geninsights/ directory structure is managed as a flat file by the agent runtime. Intended location is .geninsights/analysis/bpmn_workflows.json.

participants.name: End User
participants.description: The person who opens the browser, enters operands, selects an operation, submits the form, and reads the result or error message

participants.name: Streamlit Runtime
participants.description: The server-side Python/Streamlit execution engine that renders the UI, batches form state, executes arithmetic logic, enforces business rules, and renders output

workflows.name: Main Calculator User Journey
workflows.description: End-to-end process a user follows to perform an arithmetic calculation — from opening the application in a browser, entering operands, selecting an operation, submitting the form, through to viewing either a computed result or a division-by-zero error message. Modelled as a horizontal swimlane diagram with two participant lanes: End User and Streamlit Runtime.
workflows.trigger: User opens the Streamlit application URL in a web browser (default: http://localhost:8501)

steps.name: Open App URL
steps.description: User navigates to the Streamlit application in a web browser

steps.name: Render Calculator Form
steps.description: Streamlit renders the full page: page config (title 'Calculator', 🧮 favicon, centred layout), h1 heading, caption, two column-aligned number_input widgets (default 0.000000), operation selectbox (4 choices, default Add), and 'Calculate' submit button

steps.name: Enter First Number (num1)
steps.description: User types or adjusts the first operand. Widget enforces numeric-only entry; any finite IEEE 754 double accepted; displayed with 6 decimal places

steps.name: Enter Second Number (num2)
steps.description: User types or adjusts the second operand. Same numeric constraints apply. This value is later validated against zero when Divide is selected

steps.name: Select Operation
steps.description: User picks one of exactly four operations from the selectbox dropdown: Add, Subtract, Multiply, or Divide. Default is Add (index 0). Arbitrary values cannot be entered.

steps.name: Click 'Calculate'
steps.description: User clicks the st.form_submit_button labelled 'Calculate'. Streamlit batches all widget values (num1, num2, operation) and re-runs the full script with submitted = True

steps.name: Form Submitted?
steps.description: Exclusive gateway: evaluates the submitted boolean. If False (page load or widget change without submit), loops back to re-render the form. If True, proceeds to operation dispatch.

steps.name: Operation Dispatch
steps.description: Evaluates the operation string via if/elif/else chain and routes to the correct arithmetic branch (BR-008). Also triggers the division-by-zero guard check if Divide is selected.

steps.name: Divide AND num2 == 0?
steps.description: Exclusive gateway: only reached when operation is Divide. Checks if num2 equals exactly 0. This is the sole business validation gate in the computation pipeline.

steps.name: st.error — Division by Zero
steps.description: Renders a red Streamlit error banner: 'Division by zero is not allowed.' This is a non-exception, user-friendly Streamlit error display.

steps.name: st.stop — Halt Execution
steps.description: Calls Streamlit's st.stop() which immediately raises a StopException internally, halting all further script execution. No result, symbol, st.success, or st.expander is rendered.

steps.name: Execute Arithmetic
steps.description: Executes the correct arithmetic operation using Python native float arithmetic. Assigns result (float) and symbol (str) for the success display. Covers all four operations via the dispatch from S3.

steps.name: st.success — Result Equation
steps.description: Renders a green Streamlit success banner with the formatted equation: 'Result: {num1} {symbol} {num2} = {result}'. Uses Unicode symbols for Multiply (×) and Divide (÷).

steps.name: st.expander — Computation Details
steps.description: Renders a collapsible 'Computation details' expander containing a structured Python dict: {first_number, second_number, operation, result}. Provides transparency into the raw computed values.

steps.name: Read Error Message
steps.description: User reads the red error banner. The form remains fully visible above the error, allowing the user to correct num2 and click Calculate again.

steps.name: Review Result & Details
steps.description: User reads the green result banner showing the full equation. Optionally expands the 'Computation details' panel to inspect raw values.

steps.name: End — Error Terminal
steps.description: Process terminates in error state. User may initiate a new calculation cycle by correcting input and resubmitting.

steps.name: End — Success Terminal
steps.description: Process terminates successfully. The result equation and computation details are displayed on screen.

end_states.name: Error — Division by Zero
end_states.description: st.error banner shown; st.stop() halted execution; form remains visible for retry

end_states.name: Success — Result Displayed
end_states.description: st.success banner with equation + st.expander with computation detail dict rendered

workflows.name: Operation Dispatch Workflow
workflows.description: Detailed subprocess view of the arithmetic dispatch and result rendering that executes after form submission. Shows how the if/elif/else chain (BR-008) routes to each of the four arithmetic operations, how the division-by-zero guard (BR-001) is embedded inside the Divide branch, and how all four success paths converge at the result display node (BR-010).
workflows.trigger: submitted == True — the st.form_submit_button was clicked
workflows.diagram_type: flowchart TD with convergence node and classDef styling

steps.name: Form Submitted
steps.description: Entry point: submitted == True, all widget values batched (num1, num2, operation)

steps.name: Which Operation? (BR-008)
steps.description: Four-way exclusive gateway dispatching on the operation string value using if/elif/else. The else branch implicitly covers Divide because operation is always one of the four enumerated values (BR-003).

steps.name: Compute Addition
steps.description: result = num1 + num2; symbol = '+' (U+002B). Python native float addition.

steps.name: Compute Subtraction
steps.description: result = num1 - num2; symbol = '-' (U+002D). Python native float subtraction. Negative results are valid.

steps.name: Compute Multiplication
steps.description: result = num1 * num2; symbol = '×' (U+00D7). Python native float multiplication.

steps.name: num2 == 0? (BR-001)
steps.description: Division-by-zero guard exclusive gateway. Checked only when operation is Divide. Uses Python exact equality (IEEE 754): 0.0 == 0 is True.

steps.name: st.error — Division by Zero
steps.description: Renders red Streamlit error banner: 'Division by zero is not allowed.'

steps.name: st.stop — Halt Execution
steps.description: Immediately halts all further Streamlit script execution. No result, no symbol, no st.success, no st.expander is rendered.

steps.name: Compute Division
steps.description: result = num1 / num2; symbol = '÷' (U+00F7). Only executes when num2 != 0 (after guard passed).

steps.name: st.success — Result Equation
steps.description: Convergence point for all four arithmetic branches. Renders green Streamlit success banner: 'Result: {num1} {symbol} {num2} = {result}'. Unicode symbols used for × and ÷.

steps.name: st.expander — Computation Details
steps.description: Renders collapsible 'Computation details' panel containing structured dict: {first_number, second_number, operation, result}.

end_states.name: Error — Division by Zero
end_states.description: st.error + st.stop — execution halted before any result is produced

end_states.name: Success — Result Displayed
end_states.description: st.success equation banner and st.expander computation details rendered

workflows.name: Division-by-Zero Error Handling
workflows.description: Focused subprocess diagram for the division-by-zero guard (BR-001) within the Divide operation branch. Shows the binary split at the BR-001 gateway: the safe division path (num2 ≠ 0 → BR-007 arithmetic → BR-010 result display) versus the error path (num2 == 0 → st.error → st.stop → execution halted). Uses classDef colour styling to visually distinguish error nodes (red/pink) from success nodes (green). Includes a behavioural edge-case table for the 0/0 scenario.
workflows.trigger: operation == 'Divide' dispatched — the else branch of the BR-008 gateway is entered
workflows.diagram_type: flowchart TD with subgraph subprocess boxes and classDef styling

steps.name: Divide Operation Dispatched
steps.description: Entry: BR-008 else branch reached; operation is confirmed as Divide

steps.name: num2 == 0? (BR-001 Guard)
steps.description: Critical exclusive gateway. Evaluates Python expression: num2 == 0. Uses IEEE 754 float equality: both 0.0 and -0.0 evaluate as zero. Applies universally regardless of num1 value.

steps.name: Execute Division
steps.description: result = num1 / num2 using Python's true-division operator. symbol = '÷' (U+00F7) assigned for display. Both result and symbol are now available for the success display.

steps.name: st.success — Result Equation
steps.description: Renders green banner: 'Result: {num1} ÷ {num2} = {result}' with Unicode division symbol.

steps.name: st.expander — Computation Details
steps.description: Renders collapsible 'Computation details' dict panel.

steps.name: st.error — Division by Zero Message
steps.description: Renders Streamlit red error banner: 'Division by zero is not allowed.' No Python exception is raised; this is a user-facing UI message via Streamlit's error component.

steps.name: st.stop — Halt Script Execution
steps.description: Calls st.stop() which internally raises a StopException. All subsequent Python statements in app.py are skipped. result and symbol are never assigned. st.success and st.expander are never rendered.

steps.name: Form Remains Visible for Retry
steps.description: Because st.stop() only halts execution after the form was already rendered in this Streamlit run, the calculator form remains visible above the error banner. The user can modify num2 and click Calculate again.

steps.name: End — Division Success
steps.end_state: Success — Division result and computation details displayed

steps.name: End — Execution Halted
steps.end_state: Error — Division by Zero; st.stop() halted script; form visible for retry

end_states.name: Division Success
end_states.description: st.success equation + st.expander details rendered for the division result

end_states.name: Execution Halted
end_states.description: st.error banner shown; st.stop() halted execution; form remains visible

edge_cases.scenario: Very large number divided by zero
Division by very small non-zero number
edge_cases.outcome: Success — guard passes (any non-zero num2 is accepted)

diagram_types_used: flowchart TD with convergence node and classDef styling (PROC-002)
flowchart TD with subprocess subgraphs and classDef styling (PROC-003)
